title: BRIDGE to Congress Resolution
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Rules.
plain_summary: The BRIDGE to Congress Resolution changes the rules for how people can speak at House committee meetings. Right now, if someone wants to speak to a committee through a video call instead of showing up in person, the committee chair has to say yes first. This resolution would get rid of that rule so witnesses could appear by video without needing special permission. The change would make it easier for people to share their ideas and experiences with Congress even if they cannot travel to Washington.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Rules.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This resolution can be called the Bringing Real Ideas, Data, and Genuine Experience to Congress Resolution or the BRIDGE to Congress Resolution.

sections.title: Section 2 Elimination of Restriction That Witnesses May Appear Remotely at Committee Proceedings Only at Discretion of Chair
sections.text: This section changes the rules about witnesses appearing at committee meetings through video calls or other remote methods. Right now, witnesses can only appear remotely if the committee chair (the person who leads the committee) decides to allow it. This resolution removes that requirement by taking out the words at the discretion of the chair of the committee and from the House rules that were approved on January third, two thousand twenty five.
